top_signals.title: Crude oil jumps back above $90 as Trump says U.S. will take Iran's Kharg Island
top_signals.summary: Oil prices edged lower on Thursday after Kuwait closed its airspace and Israel warned of launches from Lebanon.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Another Gulf Producer Joins Dark-Mode Tanker Traffic Through Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Kuwait appears to have joined a growing bunch of Middle Eastern oil and gas producers that have moved to ship energy cargoes in dark mode through the Strait of Hormuz. The liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) carrier Gas Umm Al Rowaisat, which is owned by the national Kuwait Petroleum Corporation, has passed through the Strait in recent days, then transferred the cargo onto another ship which is currently en route to an Indian port, vessel-tracking data compiled by Bloomberg showed on Thursday. The Gas Umm Al Rowaisat loaded LPG in May in the Gulf, and…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: OPEC Oil Production Falls to Lowest Level Since 2000
top_signals.summary: Crude oil production among members of OPEC has this year collapsed to a generational low, at just 16.13 million barrels daily, Reuters reported, citing a regular survey it conducts on OPEC production. The amount is the lowest since 2000, the publication noted. The May average is also lower than OPEC production at the height of the Covid lockdowns, when the reduction was prompted by the collapse in demand as a result of the lockdowns. It is worth noting, however, that the May OPEC figure excludes the UAE, which left the group as of the first of…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Wärtsilä sees growing demand for flexible and future-ready vessel solutions
top_signals.summary: The maritime industry’s approach to decarbonisation is becoming increasingly pragmatic, with shipowners focusing on efficiency, flexibility and long-term asset value rather than waiting for a single fuel or technology pathway to emerge, according to Wärtsilä. Speaking to Hellenic Shipping News, Yiannis Christopoulos, General Manager Marine Sales Greece & Cyprus and Managing Director of Wärtsilä Greece, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Meloni’s staff strike against new limits on working from home
top_signals.summary: Employees in Italian prime minister’s office say secretariat buildings suffer from pest infestations and poor maintenance
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Citigroup Offers Its First Investment-Grade Bonds of 2026
top_signals.summary: Citigroup Inc. is offering its first investment-grade bonds of 2026, following $123.3 billion of issuance by the five other biggest Wall Street banks this year.

Truth bomb: Defence secretary John Healey resigns over funding battles
top_signals.summary: John Healey has resigned as defence secretary after months of tension with Sir Keir Starmer over funding for an uplift in spending on the armed forces. In a letter to the Prime Minister, Healey said the government’s draft for the Defence Investment Plan (DIP) “falls well short of what is required for defence and the [...]
